fault_hypothesis: On line 62, the mean is computed by dividing `total` by `window` instead of `len(tail)`. When `len(tail) < window` (fewer values than window width), this produces a smaller result than the true average of available data.

constraints.intent: The mean should be total divided by the actual number of tail elements, not by window.
After computing mean, mean * len(tail) should equal total when tail is shorter than window.

spec.expr: len(tail__1) == window or abs(total__1 / len(tail__1) - total__1 / window) < 1e-9

constraints.intent: Before returning mean, if tail is shorter than window the two divisors differ and the wrong one was used.

spec.expr: len(tail__1) > 0 and total__1 / len(tail__1) >= total__1 / window

constraints.intent: When tail has fewer elements than window, dividing by len(tail) gives a larger or equal mean than dividing by window.
